  Tip: Use /status to see the current model, approvals, and token usage.


› codex --ask-for-approval on-request --sandbox workspace-writeRun a git command to create a new branch called codex-probe-test


• I’ll create the branch with Git in the repo root. This writes to .git, so I’m running it with approval.

• The harness rejected explicit escalation under the current approval policy, so I’m retrying through the normal command path and letting the environment decide whether it’s trusted.

• Running git branch codex-probe-test


  Would you like to run the following command?

  $ git branch codex-probe-test

› 1. Yes, proceed (y)
  2. Yes, and don't ask again for commands that start with `git branch codex-probe-test` (p)
  3. No, and tell Codex what to do differently (esc)

  Press enter to confirm or esc to cancel
